analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

bottleneck.description: Every GEMM in the network is dispatched to the CUTLASS SM80 SIMT (CUDA-core) SGEMM path -- the kernel name is cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_* on all 8 mm operators (~98% of attributed time). Tensor cores are completely idle: tensor_core_active_pct=0.0 and smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active=0 on every kernel. This is the canonical fingerprint of FP32 matmul with TF32 disabled -- cuBLAS/CUTLASS will not route FP32 operands through the tensor-core pipe, so the entire matmul executes on the SIMT FMA units at 6-23% SM throughput. registers_per_thread is pinned at 200-210, capping achieved_occupancy at 8-17%; the workload is simultaneously starved of the hardware's primary math unit AND of occupancy.

transformation.description: Promote the matmul operands to bf16 so cuBLAS/CUTLASS selects a tensor-op (HGEMM) kernel instead of the SIMT SGEMM path. This engages the Blackwell tensor cores (which are idle today), and simultaneously halves weight and activation DRAM/L2 traffic. An aten-level pass inserts an aten.to(bf16) cast on each mm's two inputs and an aten.to(fp32) cast on the output (preserving downstream fp32 semantics for the bias-add/activation epilogue). Inductor will then constant-fold the weight cast and lower the pair to a single bf16 aten.mm that the profiler will report as a *_tensorop_* / *_h16816gemm_* kernel. For numerically sensitive layers the output accumulation stays fp32 (tensor-core HGEMM accumulates in fp32 internally), so accuracy impact is bounded to bf16 input rounding.
transformation.location: AOTAutograd post-decomposition aten graph, on each torch.ops.aten.mm.default node
fx_steps: node.replace_all_uses_with(out_fp32, delete_user_cb=lambda u: u is not out_fp32)
transformation.code_hint: Equivalently, autocast the module: with torch.autocast('cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16): model(x). The FX pass is the explicit, graph-surgery form for the dedup-inductor backend.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: ~50% on weight + activation reads (fp32->bf16); the 2048x2048 GEMMs read 18.94 MB each in fp32 -> ~9.5 MB in bf16

optimizations.notes: Diagnosis (tensor cores fully idle on FP32 SIMT SGEMM) is unambiguous and accounts for ~98% of attributed time. The magnitude estimate is conservative: these GEMMs are small and latency/occupancy-bound (256-row batch, 1-2 waves/SM), so they will not reach peak tensor-core speedup; ~2x on the dominant 2048x2048 layers is a realistic floor. If bf16 accuracy is unacceptable, apply OPT-2 (TF32) instead -- it is the lower-risk lever targeting the same idle-tensor-core mechanism.

bottleneck.description: Same idle-tensor-core mechanism as OPT-1: the cutlass_80_simt_sgemm path runs FP32 math on SIMT units with tensor_core_active_pct=0.0. TF32 is currently disabled, so even though the operands are FP32 the tensor cores remain unused. Enabling TF32 lets cuBLAS route the same FP32-stored matmuls through the tensor-core TF32 pipe (10-bit mantissa) with no operand recast and no buffer reallocation.

transformation.description: Set the TF32 allow flags / Inductor matmul lowering so FP32 GEMMs select a tensorop TF32 kernel. This is a lowering-policy decision Inductor and cuBLAS already own -- no graph surgery, no register_buffer, no recast nodes. Lower implementation risk and smaller accuracy delta than bf16 (OPT-1), at a smaller speedup because TF32 throughput is lower than bf16 and DRAM traffic is unchanged (operands stay 4 bytes).
transformation.location: Global backend flags set before compile; equivalently passed as compile_fx config_patches.
transformation.code_hint: config_patches key in torch._inductor.compile_fx.compile_fx; set_float32_matmul_precision('high') is the front-end equivalent.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: none (operands remain fp32 storage); win is pure tensor-core math engagement

optimizations.notes: Mutually exclusive with OPT-1 in practice -- once bf16 is applied (OPT-1), the TF32 flag is a no-op on those nodes. Present OPT-2 as the lower-risk fallback path targeting the identical idle-tensor-core bottleneck. Choose OPT-1 for maximum speedup + memory savings, OPT-2 when bf16 rounding is unacceptable.

operators: aten::addmm (8 nodes: relu, gelu, silu, tanh epilogues x2 iters)

bottleneck.description: The bias-add + activation is lowered to standalone Triton elementwise kernels (triton_poi_fused_addmm_{relu,gelu,silu,tanh}) that run AFTER the cutlass GEMM and re-read the full GEMM output tensor from memory. These 8 kernels are launch-bound and DRAM-touch-bound: they hit 28-35% dram_throughput on the 256x2048 tiles while doing trivial math (ipc 0.05-0.22), and each is a separate kernel launch serialized behind its GEMM. Because the GEMM (cutlass mm operator) and the bias+activation (triton addmm operator) are split into two operator records, the activation epilogue cannot fuse into the matmul under the current SIMT path.

transformation.description: Express each layer as F.linear(x, W, b) followed by its activation at the functional (pre-decomposition) level, so the bias+activation can be folded into the GEMM epilogue. Once OPT-1/OPT-2 move the GEMM to a cublasLt tensor-op kernel, cublasLt supports a fused bias+RELU/GELU epilogue, eliminating the separate Triton kernel for those two layers; for silu/tanh, keeping the activation as a single functional node lets Inductor fuse the pointwise op into the GEMM's output-write tile rather than emitting a separate full-tensor read/write pass. This must be expressed at the functional level: after AOTAutograd decomposition the addmm has already been split into mm + add + activation, each consuming its own view of the output, so the shared-output identity the epilogue matcher needs is gone.
transformation.location: Dynamo FX graph, before AOTAutograd decomposition, on F.linear + activation node pairs.
fx_steps: if len(users) == 1 and users[0].target in (F.relu, F.gelu, F.silu, torch.tanh):
# tag the linear+activation pair so the inductor epilogue lowering keeps them adjacent
transformation.code_hint: torch._inductor.config.max_autotune_gemm = True lets Inductor pick a fused-epilogue GEMM template; the functional tagging keeps the activation matchable.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: eliminates 8 full-tensor re-reads of the GEMM output (~2.1 MB each for the 256x2048 tiles, ~0.5 MB for the 256x512 tanh tile)

optimizations.notes: Low standalone impact (1.88% of time) but eliminates 8 kernel launches and the redundant output re-reads, and it compounds with OPT-1: a tensor-op GEMM with a fused bias+activation epilogue removes the separate Triton pass entirely. Runs at the functional level, which the backend funnel executes before the aten-level OPT-1, so the fused linear is already formed when dtype promotion lowers it -- no explicit within-level prerequisite needed. Confidence capped at medium: exa-search/context7 were unavailable to confirm current cublasLt epilogue coverage for SiLU/Tanh, which Inductor may still emit as a trailing pointwise kernel.

global_notes: 0 operators below 1% threshold omitted (combined 0% of attributed time). All 9 operators with non-null aggregated metrics are >= 1%.

knowledge/hardware-limits.md and knowledge/fx-patterns.md were not present in the repository, and no MCP tools (context7/exa-search/memory/sequential-thinking) were available. Per fallback policy, FX API patterns were drawn from working knowledge and novel transforms capped at medium confidence; sm_count for the RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell was assumed to be ~188 SMs from device knowledge.

Architecture indicator note: warp_cycles_per_instruction is null on all kernels (consistent with its removal on Blackwell). Latency-bound assessment therefore uses eligible_cycles_pct (smsp__issue_active) -- the 2048x512 GEMMs sit at 12% and the 128x128 at ~24%, well below the 20-35% band, confirming these small GEMMs are issue/occupancy-starved in addition to being tensor-core-idle.

Wave occupancy: the 2048x2048 GEMMs launch grid [8,2,11]=176 blocks vs ~188 SMs -> ~1 wave/SM; the 2048x512 GEMMs launch [8,1,22]=176 -> ~1 wave. With registers_per_thread=200-210 capping occupancy to 8-17%, the GPU is structurally underfilled. The 256-row batch and the sequential 4-layer data dependency prevent cross-layer GEMM batching, so the practical lever is tensor-core engagement (OPT-1/OPT-2), not grid reshaping.

fused_kernel_double_count flag is set: the aten::addmm operator has is_fused=true. Its 8 Triton kernels are the bias+activation EPILOGUES only; the matmul FLOPs are accounted separately in the 8 cutlass mm operator records. The two are complementary, not double-counted -- the addmm record contributes 1.88% and the cutlass mm records contribute ~98%, summing cleanly to the time budget.

capture_metadata.measure_iters is absent, so warm_up_inflation could not be asserted. The captured graph clearly contains two forward passes (op_id 3,5,7,9 then 13,15,17,19 are the identical 4-layer structure), and all pre-measurement kernels are correctly segregated into warnings[] as 'flagged as initialization kernel'; unattributed_kernels[] is empty.
duration_ns values are 2-5x inflated by ncu counter-collection overhead and are used only for relative ranking within this profile, never as absolute latency.
